methods.description: The sediment samples were analyzed for various properties, including total organic carbon (TOC), total nitrogen (TN), ammonia nitrogen (NH4+-N), nitrate nitrogen (NO3−-N), and inorganic phosphorous (IP). Additionally, the specific activity of 226Ra and 210Pb was measured, and the age of sediment deposition was calculated.

steps.description: The TOC was detected using a TOC analyzer (TOC-L&SSM, Shimadzu Corp, Japan) with the dry combustion method.
steps.searchable_summary: measure TOC using TOC analyzer with dry combustion method

steps.description: The TN was measured using an ultraviolet spectrophotometer (UV 3600, Shimadzu Corp, Japan) with the sulfate digestion method.
steps.searchable_summary: measure TN using UV spectrophotometer with sulfate digestion

steps.description: The NH4+-N and nitrate nitrogen NO3−-N were measured using a continuous flow analyzer (Skalar, Breda, Netherlands).
steps.searchable_summary: measure NH4+-N and NO3−-N using continuous flow analyzer

steps.description: The IP was measured following the Standards Measurements and Testing (SMT) protocol.
The specific activity of 226Ra and 210Pb was measured using a high-resolution high purity Germanium (HPGe) γ-spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure 226Ra and 210Pb activity using HPGe γ-spectrometer

steps.description: The age of the sediment deposition was calculated using the constant rate of supply (CRS) of the 210Pb model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sediment age using CRS of 210Pb model

methods.description: Networks were built based on all samples in each lake to explore co-occurrence patterns among the microbial community. The networks were analyzed to understand the distribution and interaction of microbial communities in different sediment layers.

steps.description: Networks were built based on all samples in each lake to explore co-occurrence patterns among the microbial community.
steps.searchable_summary: build co-occurrence networks from microbial community samples

steps.description: The network topology indices were analyzed to understand the distribution of OTUs in different sediment layers.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze network topology indices for OTU distribution

steps.description: The degree and betweenness centrality of abundant OTUs were compared to rare and moderate OTUs.
steps.searchable_summary: compare degree and betweenness centrality of OTUs

steps.description: Positive and negative interactions among OTUs were calculated, with a focus on interactions in different depth layers.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate positive and negative OTU interactions
